articles.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
articles.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection, and it is separate

keypoints: 400+ AUR packages compromised with credential-stealing malware
Rust infostealer loads eBPF rootkit for persistence

analysis.desc: Attackers compromised over 400 community packages simultaneously, affecting developers building from AUR repository.

analysis.desc: Rust-based infostealer harvests credentials, escalates to root, deploys eBPF rootkit for advanced hiding.

analysis.title: AUR trust model broken
analysis.desc: Community-maintained packages lack centralized security screening, enabling widespread build-time code injection attacks.

ai_summary.suggested_title: AUR Packages Hijacked — Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit Deployed
ai_summary.bottom_line: If you build from AUR, your developer credentials are likely already compromised.
ai_summary.action_today: Arch Linux → pacman -Syu && aur audit (check package integrity immediately)

Cybersecurity is full of work that deserves recognition and rarely gets it. Products that quietly close real gaps. Teams that stop incidents nobody reads about. Companies that raise the

keypoints: 95 cybersecurity award categories recognize invisible security work
Recognition celebrates products and teams preventing unreported incidents

analysis.title: Awards Honor Hidden Security Heroes
analysis.desc: Recognition program acknowledges security professionals whose work prevents incidents that never make headlines or public awareness.

analysis.title: Four Main Award Categories Span Industry
analysis.desc: 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards distribute recognition across 95 subcategories within four primary award categories recognizing diverse security contributions.

articles.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Worm Code Leaked, AI Agent Phished, Claude Code Patch + 28 New Stories
articles.summary: It's been one of those weeks. You expect the usual noise: recycled malware, sloppy attacks, another easy target getting hit. Instead, there's a supply chain attack kit in a public repo, a $5,000-a-month RAT that clones browsers, and research showing AI agents can be tricked into leaking real credentials. The bigger problem is how polished this all looks now. Mule networks run like SaaS.

keypoints: Supply chain attack kits publicly leaked and accessible
AI agents tricked into exposing real user credentials

analysis.title: Professionalized cybercrime infrastructure
analysis.desc: Criminal operations now resemble legitimate SaaS platforms with polished mule networks and $5K/month RAT tools cloning browsers.

analysis.title: AI model security weakness
analysis.desc: Claude and similar agents vulnerable to social engineering attacks designed to extract credentials and sensitive information.

analysis.title: Public repository risks
analysis.desc: Complete supply chain attack toolkits discovered in open repositories, enabling mass adoption by lower-skill threat actors.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Supply Chain Attacks, AI Exploits, Claude Patch Released
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your credentials are being harvested by machines smarter than you think.
ai_summary.action_today: Gmail → Security → 2-Step Verification → Enable now (5 minutes)

articles.title: Coupang hit with record $409 million data breach fine in Korea
articles.summary: ​​The Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC), South Korea's data protection regulator, has fined e-commerce giant Coupang a record 624.6 billion won (roughly $409 million) following a massive data breach affecting more than 37 million customers [...]

keypoints: Record $409M fine for 37M customer data breach
South Korea's PIPC enforces strict data protection penalties

analysis.title: Scale of exposure
analysis.desc: 37 million Coupang customers compromised in massive breach affecting personal information stored by e-commerce platform.

analysis.title: Regulatory enforcement
analysis.desc: Record fine demonstrates South Korea's aggressive stance on data protection violations and corporate accountability standards.

analysis.title: Industry implications
analysis.desc: Penalty sets precedent for global tech companies operating in Korea, signaling rising costs of security negligence.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Data Breach Fine Record: Coupang Faces $409M Penalty
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your Coupang account data is already in the wild—assume it's compromised.
ai_summary.action_today: Check email for Coupang breach notification, change password immediately if account exists

articles.title: AI Broke Vulnerability Management. That's Why CISOs Are Moving Budget to BAS.
articles.summary: For thirty years, vulnerability management ran on a buffer: the months between when a vulnerability was found and when someone could figure out how to weaponize it. The solution was straightforward enough; triage by severity, schedule the fix, validate, and move on. The buffer was what made that work. Today, that buffer is gone. AI didn't make your team slower. It changed the other side of the

keypoints: AI accelerated exploit development, eliminating disclosure buffer
CISOs shifting budgets from VM to breach simulation testing

analysis.title: The Vulnerability Window Collapsed
analysis.desc: AI-powered tools compressed exploit development timelines from months to days, making traditional vulnerability triage workflows obsolete.

analysis.title: Traditional VM Now Insufficient
analysis.desc: Severity scoring and patching schedules no longer protect against rapid weaponization, forcing security leaders to rethink strategies.

analysis.title: BAS Becomes Priority Investment
analysis.desc: Breach and attack simulation testing now receives budget reallocation as organizations shift focus from reactive patching to proactive resilience.

ai_summary.suggested_title: AI Killed Vulnerability Buffers — CISOs Pivot to Breach Simulation
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your patching schedule is already outdated before the patch ships.
ai_summary.action_today: Schedule breach simulation assessment with your security team this week
